lines.speaker: Produced by
lines.text: Robert Carlock & Wendy Knoller
Final check by Kim
Chandler and Monica's apartment. They are having a diner party with Phoebe and Mike.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: (raising his glass) Thank you guys for having us over.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh! Yeah, this is fun, couples night.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, I don't know why we hang out with married couples more often.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, because every time we do, you make jokes about swinging and scare them away.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You mean that Portuguese couple? Yeah, like you wouldn't have done it. (she shrugs)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (entering) Hey, you guys... I have great news.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ross, we're kind of in the middle of diner here.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, well, er, I already ate, but sure...! (they all look at each other when Ross grabs a plate) Guess what happened at work today...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: A dinosaur died a million years ago?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Try sixty-five million years ago, and then try sssshhhhhh.... My tenure review board met today and I hear it's looking really good.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wow!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah. Do you have any idea what this means in academic circles, uh? I am gonna get laid.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (while entering) Hi you guys.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ooh, Italian! (she also grabs a plate)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No one wanted seconds, right?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, no. I-I'm good.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey you guys... You're never gonna believe it. This headhunter called me. I have a meeting tomorrow with Gucci. Gucci wants me.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm up for tenure.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Congratulations!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You too! What are the odds?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ooh! (they hug)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (enters) Guess what? (they all look expectantly at him) I finally got that seed out of my teeth.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I don't know who I'm happiest for...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I do, he's been working on that all day! (looking at Joey)
OPENING CREDITS
Central Perk. Phoebe, Monica and Chandler on their couch.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey Mon? Was it weird changing your name to Geller-Bing?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, no. It felt nice to acknowledge this. (pats Chandler on his leg)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Where did you go to do it?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Uhm the... the ministry... of names... bureau...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: YOU NEVER DID IT!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'm sorry. It's just the idea of being an official Bing.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey! I will have you know that... aah, who am I kidding. Let's call the kid Geller and let Bing die with me.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: (walks to the couch with coffee for Phoebe) Here you go.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Thanks! Honey, would you want me to take your name?

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Oh, it's just... It's up to you. It's your name. You've got to live with it.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All right, let's see, call me mrs Hannigan.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Mrs Hannigan?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What? Can't you see I'm in the middle of something? Ooh, I like it.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (enters) Hey guys.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey Joe! We've got a couple of things we've got to check out at the new house. You want to come with us?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, thank you.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right. I know you're not happy about us moving, but you're the only one who hasn't seen the house.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, come with us. You'll see how close it is to the city.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: But no, it's not close. You said it was in escrow? I couldn't even find it on the map.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Joey, please come. It would mean so much to us.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You know what? You are my friends, I wanna be supportive, I will come with you. SHOTGUN!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Damn it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: See you guys later.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (to Joey) I'll pick you up at eleven. So glad you're coming.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Good for you. That was really mature.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What? No, the only reason I'm going to their stupid new house, is so I can point out everything that's wrong with it, so they don't move. I'm gonna make them stay here.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: You're a strange kind of grown-up.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Joey, you can't make someone do something they don't want to do. Believe me, there's something I've been trying to get Mike to do in bed and there's... he's just...

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Woo-wo-hey-hey-hey... Can we not talk about that right now?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All right, prude... Look, Monica and Chandler really love this house. You are not gonna talk them into staying here.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, hey... I can convince people to do anything, you know. I bet I can even get Mike to do that "thing". What is it?
Phoebe whispers something in Joey's ear, but after hearing it he jumps up, shocked

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I AM NOT GONNA HELP YOU DO THAT! Goodbye! (he leaves)
A restaurant. Rachel enters.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (to maitre d') Hi, I'm here to see mr Campbell... with Gucci. The reservation is probably under Gucci. It's spelled like Gukki, which could be confusing.

lines.speaker: Maitre d'
lines.text: Mr Campbell's not here yet. Let me show you to his table.
They walk to the table, but Rachel suddenly gasps. Sitting there is mr Zellner, her boss from her current job at Ralph Lauren.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God! That's my boss. You have to seat us somewhere else.

lines.speaker: Maitre d'
lines.text: I'm sorry. That's always mr Campbell's table.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: But my... but my boss cannot see me. I'm interviewing for another job.

lines.speaker: Maitre d'
lines.text: I know. With Gukki

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Sssshhhh!

lines.speaker: Mr Zellner
lines.text: Rachel?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi... I'm on a date...

lines.speaker: Mr Zellner
lines.text: (confused) That's great!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, it is. Yeah, you know, it's tough. Single mom, career... You gotta get out there.

lines.speaker: Mr Zellner
lines.text: Well, you got uhm... good energy.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Mr Campbell
lines.text: Rachel?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes, hi!

lines.speaker: Mr Campbell
lines.text: James Campbell...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi! (to mr Zellner) Excuse us.

lines.speaker: Mr Campbell
lines.text: Please... (shows her to sit)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay. Oh, yeah... (whispering to mr Zellner) Oh he's cute!

lines.speaker: Mr Campbell
lines.text: So... your resume is quite impressive. (Mr Zellner who sits behind Rachel shrugs)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wha... My resume? I wouldn't... I wouldn't call my online dating profile a resume.

lines.speaker: Mr Campbell
lines.text: Dating profile? I-I-I'm talking about the work resume.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (starts singing la la la la) Whatever happened to just singing for no reason? Huh?

lines.speaker: Mr Campbell
lines.text: Maybe people... found it weird.... So, why do you want to leave Ralph Lauren?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What? I-I don't.

lines.speaker: Mr Campbell
lines.text: You don't?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, I-I-I love it there.

lines.speaker: Mr Campbell
lines.text: Well, if you don't want to leave, why are we having this lunch?
Rachel mimes and mouths to mr Campbell "That is my boss", pointing to mr Zellner

lines.speaker: Mr Campbell
lines.text: What?
Rachel now silently whispers "That's my boss".

lines.speaker: Mr Campbell
lines.text: That's Hugo Boss?
Rachel holds her hand in order to support her head. Mr Zellner obviously overheard the conversation.
A counter at a government building. Phoebe's waiting in line.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (to the woman behind her) This place is so depressing. If I had to work here I'd kill myself. (she turns around and the clerk behind the counter heard her.) But you obviously haven't.

lines.speaker: Clerk
lines.text: How can I help you?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I need to change my name, please. See, I need to change it because I'm-I'm hiding from the law. (the clerk shows no change in expression whatsoever) You're fun.

lines.speaker: Clerk
lines.text: You need to fill out this form. (motions for the next person in line)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, well, I just don't, I don't know how it works exactly. See, my name is Buffay and my husband's name is Hannigan, so is it supposed to be Buffay-Hannigan or Hannigan-Buffay?

lines.speaker: Clerk
lines.text: It can be anything you want.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, not anything, I mean...

lines.speaker: Clerk
lines.text: Yeah... anything.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, this could take a while.

lines.speaker: Clerk
lines.text: Get out of my line.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay.
Central Perk. Chandler and Monica are there when Phoebe enters.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey Pheebs.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, not anymore. I changed it today.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, I'm sorry, mrs Hannigan.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wrong again! Apparently you can change it to anything you want. So I thought, all right, here's an opportunity to be creative. So meet Princess Consuela Banana Hammock.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That's what we were gonna name the baby.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Phoebe!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Uh! Princess Consuela.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You seriously changed your name to that?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Uh-huh!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, so from now on we have to call you Princess Consuela?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Uhm, no. I'm gonna have my friends call me Valerie.
Rachel enters, looking depressed

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, how'd the interview go?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh! It's not good.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You know, I always feel that way after an interview. I'll bet it went better than you think.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, I didn't get the job at Gucci and I got fired from Ralph Lauren.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That is a bad interview.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What are you, what are you talking about? How did this happen?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, my boss was at the same restaurant where I was having my interview and he heard everything. So later he calls me to his office and he tells me that he's gonna have to let me go, because I'm not a team player. And I said "Wait a minute! Yes I am." and I had to sit there for 45 minutes while he proved that that in fact... was true.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh God. I'm so sorry.
Ross enters with a bottle in his hand

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey! Wha-hoo! What's this? (showing the bottle) Well it's a, it's a bottle of champagne. Why is this here?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ross...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I guess it's here because I GOT TENURE!
All (except Rachel): Congratulations! (Rachel looks devastated

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: This is the single greatest day of my professional career. Gunther, six glasses!

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: Six? You want me to join you?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, I thought Joey was here. Five is good. (Gunther leaves, hurt) Well, I'm gonna have a loogie in my coffee tomorrow.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ooh! Israeli champagne. And it's vanilla!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I got tenure. I didn't win the lottery... Hey Rach, so uh... how did your thing go?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh it... good! Yeah, but I'm not gonna hear from that for a couple of days.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, you know what? You're gonna get it. I-I-I-I can feel it.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Can you?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ah, all right. Here's to Ross!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: And-and to years of hard work finally paying off.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: And to knowing that your career doesn't mean everything. (Rachel mouths "aah")

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: But also knowing it means a lot.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: But more importantly to full well-rounded lives.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: ...that center around work.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: To Ross!
All (except Rachel): Ross!
they all drink from the champagne, but clearly dislike the taste of it

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You know what the best part about this is? I can never be fired.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh God!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No seriously. I have job security for life. You know, I never have to worry. (Rachel starts crying) Oh, look at you. Look how happy you are for me.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (crying) No, it's not that. I got fired today. And I didn't get the other job.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Rach, I'm so sorry.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Great. I feel like an idiot.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, it's okay, you didn't know.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh... (to the others) Little heads-up would have been nice.
Chandler and Monica's future house. They enter the living room with the realtor and Joey.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Thank you for letting us see the house again.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And thank you for explaining to us what escrow means... I've already forgotten what you said, but thank you.

lines.speaker: Realtor
lines.text: Take as long as you want. Just let me know when you're through. (she leaves the room)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ah, so glad you decided to come.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Me too. Yeah, this place is great. I'm so happy for you guys. Although, you know, I hope you like fungus.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Fungus! Yeah. Place is full of it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No it's not. We had an inspection and they didn't find anything.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay. Then I guess I have dry eyes and a scratchy throat for no reason.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Maybe because it's you hung your head out of the window like a dog the whole ride here.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Maybe. So this is the living room huh? Ooh, it's pretty dark. (starts feeling around him like he's in a completely dark room, touching Chandler, who backs out and hits him)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No it's not!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (squinting his eyes) Are you kiddin'? I think I just saw a bat in the corner!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: When your head was hanging out the window, it didn't hit a mailbox, did it?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (glares at him for a moment, then admits grudgingly) Maybe. Well, I just think you guys can do better than this house, you know? Or any other house for that matter.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh Joey, look, we know you're having a hard time with this, but we really, we love it here.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: FINE, ok, if you love this house so much, then you should just live here, okay? I just hope you get used to that weird humming sound. (He turns his back to them and starts humming) Hmmmmmmmmmmmmmm...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Joey, we know that's you.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: no... hmmmmm... it's not... hmmmmmmmmmm
Phoebe is at Central Perk. Mike enters.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Hey (He kisses Phoebe)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Welcome back!

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Ah! I missed you

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, me too!

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: So, what's new?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, I'm no longer Phoebe Buffay.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: That's great! You changed you name?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yes I did! Meet: Princess Consuela Banana Hammock! (She smiles from ear to ear)

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: (afraid) You're kidding right?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Nope.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: You really did that?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yep.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Yeah, but you can't do that.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Why? It's fun, it's different, no-one else has a name like it.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: (looks at her astonished) Alright, then I'm gonna change my name.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Great, okay, what are you gonna change it to?

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Crap Bag.
Phoebe:(not amused) Mike Crap Bag?

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: No, no Mike, just Crap Bag. First name Crap, last name Bag.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You're not serious, right?

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Yeah, I'm serious. (sarcastic) It's fun, it's different and no-one else has a name like that!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Uhu, uhu, well, then, great. If you love it, I love it.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: I do love it, and I love your name. I love Princess Consuela.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: And I love Crap.
Joey is in Monica and Chandler's future house, sitting in a child's bedroom, looking at a quiz card which has "5+10=" printed on one side.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (turns the card around, obviously had the wrong answer) Ow!
A young girl enters

lines.speaker: Girl
lines.text: Who are you?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, hi, I'm Joey. My stupid friends are buying this house. Who are you?

lines.speaker: Girl
lines.text: I'm Mackenzie. My stupid parents are selling this house.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (understanding) Oh.

lines.speaker: Mackenzie
lines.text: (sighs) I hate my parents.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I hate my friends. (They shake on it as if they just made a pact) Alright, look. There's gotta be a way that we can stop this from happening.

lines.speaker: Mackenzie
lines.text: Like what?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (thinking) Uhm... oh! Okay. You come with me, and you tell them that the house is haunted!

lines.speaker: Mackenzie
lines.text: What are you? Eight?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Woah, uh! Okay, let's hear your great idea.

lines.speaker: Mackenzie
lines.text: I don't have any great ideas. I am eight.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (frustrated) Ahh! There's gotta be a way. I mean, you know, if Monica and Chandler move out here and now Phoebe is married to Mike. That just leaves me and Ross and Rach, you know what I mean?

lines.speaker: Mackenzie
lines.text: I really don't.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What am I gonna do, I feel like I'm losing my friends.

lines.speaker: Mackenzie
lines.text: My parents say I'm gonna make new friends.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, yeah, sure, easy for you, you're young. Me, I'm set in my ways.

lines.speaker: Mackenzie
lines.text: This is what my mom was talking about. Whiners are wieners. (Joey glares at her angrily) Look, you want your friends to be happy, right?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, yeah, I guess.

lines.speaker: Mackenzie
lines.text: Well, if moving here is gonna make them happy, don't you want them to do it?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (having difficulty admitting it) Yeah, maybe.

lines.speaker: Mackenzie
lines.text: Then you gotta let them go.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (In near tears, realizes she is right) I hate to admit it, but you're probably right. How did you get to be so smart?

lines.speaker: Mackenzie
lines.text: I read a lot.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (his mood changes instantly) Just when I thought we could be friends. (he leaves the room)
Outside Ralph Lauren building. Rachel just walked out carrying a box of her stuff, and a strange man approaches her.

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: Hey Rach, I just heard. I'm so sorry.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, thank you... (looks at his face trying to remember his name)

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: You still don't know my name, do you?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (Is embarrassed for a moment, but it quickly passes) Well, now I don't have to. (The man leaves instantly)
In the meantime, Ross is trying to squeeze and push a rather large chair through the revolving doors of the Ralph Lauren building.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (annoyed) Ross, what is taking you so long?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (stares at her through the door and starts pushing the chair harder, looking very annoyed. He finally manages) (sarcastic) I'm sorry, it's almost as if this wasn't built for a quick getaway!
Mark, approaches from behind and recognizes her

lines.speaker: Mark
lines.text: Rachel?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (turns around) Mark? Oh my God! (puts the box on the chair and they hug each other)

lines.speaker: Mark
lines.text: How've you been?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I'm fantastic. You remember Ross?

lines.speaker: Mark
lines.text: Sure, sure. (To Ross) What's with the chair. (Rachel signals him not to mention she's been fired)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh, you know, you can't always get a seat on the subway, so... (laughs stupidly)

lines.speaker: Mark
lines.text: Clever. (back to Rachel) So how are you?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, well, (looks at her box and chair) you're not catching me on my best day.

lines.speaker: Mark
lines.text: Yeah, a box full of your desk stuff doesn't exactly say big promotion.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, but it's good, you know, I'm gonna take some time off and do some charity work.

lines.speaker: Mark
lines.text: Are you sure, because we may have something at Louis Vuitton.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, screw charity work. What've you got?

lines.speaker: Mark
lines.text: Why don't we have dinner tonight and talk about it?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Great! I'll call ya!

lines.speaker: Mark
lines.text: (shakes Ross' hand) Nice to see you.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah! yeah, I got tenure! (Mark looks at him strangely and walks off)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (very excited) Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: See? I told you something good would come along. And he seemed really nice. I've met him before?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross! That's Mark. From Bloomingdales? You were insanely jealous of him.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (realizing) That is Mark?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I hate that guy.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, no, NO, you cannot go to dinner with him.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What? You don't want me to get a job?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh yeah, I'm sure he's gonna give you a job. Maybe make you his SEXretary.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ugh.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm serious. I just don't trust that guy, okay?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross, you know what? (looks over to the door and sees security staring at them) Okay, let's talk about it later, there comes security. (Takes her box and leaves. Ross follows her and then returns for the chair. He stands for a moment, then pushes it quickly in the general direction Rachel went into, and out of the camera's view, and then nonchalantly walks away)
Chandler and Monica's new house. Sitting near the window, they look at the neighborhood.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, I love this street. The trees, the big front yards, the actual picket fences.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Man, those two dogs are going at it!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (entering) Hey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey, where have you been?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, just er... you know, looking around. But you know what? This house... is great.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Really? What changed your mind?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh well, the little girl who lives here made me feel a lot better about the whole thing.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Joey, there was a little girl who lived here, but she died like 30 years ago.
Joey's eyes double in size

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (frightened) What?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ha! I'm just messing with you.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That's not funny! You know I'm afraid of little girl ghosts!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Joey, now that you're okay with the house, do you wanna go see your room?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What? I get my own room?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You don't think we'd buy a house and not have a Joey room do you?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh my God! (they all hug) Oh! Hey, can I have an aquarium? And a sex swing?

lines.speaker: Chandler and Monica
lines.text: No!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Why not? I'll keep the tank clean.
Central Perk. Phoebe and Mike are leaving.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: After you, miss Banana Hammock.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Thank you, mister Bag.
a woman enters and recognizes Phoebe

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Oh hey, how are you?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh hi Rita! Good! (to Mike) Oh, Rita's a massage client.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Oh! Why don't you introduce me?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (shrugs) Er, Rita, this is my husband.

lines.speaker: Rita
lines.text: Oh! (they shake hands)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Why don't you tell her my name?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (without moving her lips, wearing a fake smile) Okay, I will. (to Rita) This is my husband Crap Bag.

lines.speaker: Rita
lines.text: Crap Bag?

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: If you need an easy way to remember it, just think of a bag of crap.

lines.speaker: Rita
lines.text: Okay. Excuse me...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah... Ogh... Okay, fine. You made your point. Can you please just be Mike Hannigan again?

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Only if you'll be Phoebe Buffay.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: How about uhm... How about Buffay-Hannigan?

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah. I'm Phoebe Buffay-Hannigan Banana Hammock.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Do you even know what a banana hammock is?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It's a funny word.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: It's a Speedo.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: ...Oh crap!
Monica and Chandler's apartment. Ross enters.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, is Rachel here?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: She's still at dinner?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I guess. Why? Who's she with?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That guy Mark. From Bloomingdale's... She thinks he's just being nice to her. But I know he really wants to sleep with her.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It's seven years ago. (he looks surprised) My time machine works!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: We ran into him on the street today and he said he might have a job for her. But I know he just wants to get into her pants.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So what if he wants to sleep with her? I mean, she's single and he's cute.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Excuse me?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh please! Yesterday on the subway? You couldn't stop staring at that woman with the big breasts the whole time.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: For your information, I was staring at her baby. We're about to be parents.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, sorry!

She looks at Ross, a bit ashamed. Chandler mimes "big breasts" to Ross and lip syncs "Wow". Ross looks at him, astonished and then Monica looks at Chandler again. A little too late he changes the "big breasts" mime into "rocking a baby". When he realizes Monica might have seen it he also strokes his imaginary baby's head.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (entering) Hi you guys!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, so uhm... How was dinner?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, it was great. Mark is so sweet.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (speaking without pause, agitated) Oh yeah? Yeah? I wonder why? What could that smarmy letch possibly want?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh Ross, come on. He's happily married. His wife just had twins.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Should we send something?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: How did the job stuff go?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: He offered me one.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That's great!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Congratulations!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I know, it's amazing. It's amazing. It's so much better than what I had at Ralph Lauren. The money is great...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Can we, can we just stop for a second? Who said something better would come along, huh? You didn't believe me. I told you everything was gonna work out. (gasps) You know what? This calls for a bottle of Israels finest.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: The job is in Paris. (they all stare at each other)
COMMERCIAL BREAK
Joey and Rachel's. Joey's on the phone.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I mean, this soap opera is a great gig, but... am I missing opportunities? You know, I've always thought of myself as a serious actor. I mean, should I be trying to do more independent movies?

lines.speaker: Mackenzie
lines.text: (at the other end of the line) I don't know... You know what? I'm gonna put you on with my bear. Hold on. (she puts the phone at the bears ear)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey bear, I need some career advice.
THE END
